rubric_items.reasoning: The stage's job (etl-job) shows Complete 1/1 with the pipeline's own success marker ('spark_etl_pipeline_verified') in its terminal logs — this is strong indirect evidence the underlying fix (driver/executor memory raised to 1g, matching the task's explicitly prescribed remedy) is still in effect, since the job would OOM again in Stage 3 if memory had reverted to 256m. The agent's reasoning about spark-worker staying at 1/1 being expected (the job runs in local[2] mode and doesn't depend on the worker Deployment for execution) is technically correct per the task's own description and matches that worker scaling was explicitly optional. This is a durable, root-cause-correct outcome, not a lucky pass.

The agent recognized this was a continuation of already-completed work, checked job/pod/deployment status first, pulled the tail of the job log to confirm the completion marker, then reported findings — a short, direct, and logically ordered verification path with no backtracking or wasted steps.

All 7 calls were scoped, read-only kubectl commands (get job, get pods, get deployment, logs) confined to the spark-etl namespace — exactly the minimal inspection needed to confirm state, with zero mutations.

The agent made no changes at all, correctly judging that the prior stage's fix already satisfied the task and that the worker replica count required no restoration. This is the smallest possible footprint for a verification-only stage.
